title: Eyelid swelling
source_review_basis: committed exact-section summaries and evidence records; full guideline documents were not retained for this pass

presenting_history.text: Supports clinician-documented eyelid irritation, redness, swelling, discharge, pain context, stye or hordeolum identity, typical eyelid-lump appearance, and selected risk-factor fields.
presenting_history.rationale: Supports eyelid irritation, redness, swelling, discharge, pain and lump context for eyelid swelling; no decision is generated.
presenting_history.evidence_extract: Supports clinician-documented eyelid irritation, redness, swelling, discharge, pain context, stye or hordeolum identity, typical eyelid-lump appearance, and selected risk-factor fields.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Swollen Eyelid – 40

exact_section.heading: Sections 1.2 and 5.1 — common eyelid symptoms and styes

presenting_history.text: Supports blepharitis identity, watery or red eyes, gritty, burning, stinging, greasy, itchy, swollen or crusted eyelids, light sensitivity, eyelid observation, and clinician-documented eyelid-hygiene discussion.
presenting_history.rationale: Supports blepharitis symptoms and eyelid observation for eyelid swelling; no decision is generated.
presenting_history.evidence_extract: Supports blepharitis identity, watery or red eyes, gritty, burning, stinging, greasy, itchy, swollen or crusted eyelids, light sensitivity, eyelid observation, and clinician-documented eyelid-hygiene discussion.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Swollen Eyelid – 40

referral.text: Supports clinician-led referral, worsening, vision change, severe redness, severe pain, photophobia, spread, trauma, uncertain diagnosis, and refractory-symptom documentation without automatically concluding urgency.

source_limitations: All item-level mappings remain unsupported pending separate clinician review and signed approval.
Lid and eye, onset, pain, itch, discharge, fever, vision, movement, trauma, examination, diagnosis and clinician plan are absent.
No diagnosis, interpretation, investigation, treatment, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, escalation or disposition is generated for eyelid swelling.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
